statement: Embedded runtime code must not call new, delete, malloc, free, or any dynamic allocation function.
enforcement: Manual review. clang-tidy rules cppcoreguidelines-no-malloc, hicpp-no-malloc, cppcoreguidelines-owning-memory are not yet enabled — enabling them in .clang-tidy is a recommended follow-up.
mitigation: Code reviewer must flag any heap allocation found in the embedded runtime paths listed in scope. Enable clang-tidy ownership rules to automate.
violationConsequence: Heap allocation in embedded runtime causes non-deterministic latency spikes, fragmentation, and potential hard-fault at runtime on MCUs with limited SRAM.

statement: Embedded runtime code must not use std::make_unique or std::make_shared.
enforcement: Manual review. See i001 for planned clang-tidy automation.
violationConsequence: Same as i001 — these helpers allocate on the heap.

statement: Embedded runtime code must not use std::vector, std::string, std::deque, std::list, std::map, or std::set. Use infra::BoundedVector, infra::BoundedString, infra::BoundedDeque, infra::BoundedList, or std::array instead.
enforcement: Manual review. A grep for '<vector>|<string>|<deque>|<list>|<map>|<set>' in the scoped paths can be added to CI.
mitigation: Search for dynamic STL container includes under the scoped paths before merge. Replace with infra::Bounded* equivalents.
violationConsequence: Dynamic STL containers heap-allocate internally, defeating the no-heap requirement and making stack usage non-deterministic.

statement: Embedded runtime code must not use recursion. Call stacks must be statically bounded.
enforcement: Manual review. No automated stack-depth analysis tool is configured yet.
mitigation: Reviewer must trace call graphs from ISR entry points to verify no recursive paths exist.
violationConsequence: Unbounded stack depth causes stack overflow on MCUs with small stack regions, resulting in silent memory corruption or hard-fault.

statement: Interfaces must not declare pure virtual destructors (virtual ~X() = 0 is forbidden). The default is no pure virtual destructor.
scope: core/foc/interfaces/, core/platform_abstraction/, all abstract base classes
enforcement: Manual review by reviewer agent checklist.
mitigation: If a pure virtual destructor appears in a review, flag as CRITICAL. Only add one when there is a proven, documented need for polymorphic deletion through the base pointer.
violationConsequence: Pure virtual destructors pull in __cxa_pure_virtual and additional vtable overhead, increasing flash and RAM consumption on resource-constrained MCUs.

statement: The FOC Calculate() method must complete in fewer than 400 cycles at 120 MHz for the 20 kHz control rate.
enforcement: Documentation requirement. Cycle count must be measured and recorded in documentation/performance-optimization/README.md for any change to the hot path.
mitigation: Use arm-none-eabi-objdump -d -C to inspect generated assembly. Measure cycle count on target or simulator before and after any hot-path change.
violationConsequence: Exceeding the cycle budget causes FOC loop overrun, triggering a fault state and stopping the motor.

statement: Virtual dispatch must not occur inside the Calculate() hot path or any ISR-reachable code path.
enforcement: Manual review. Reviewer must verify no virtual call sites appear in the hot path via code inspection or assembly review.
mitigation: Use concrete types or templates for hot-path collaborators. If a vtable call cannot be avoided, document it with a cycle-count measurement.
violationConsequence: Each virtual dispatch adds 2–5 cycles minimum and defeats branch-prediction; at 20 kHz this compounds across millions of iterations.

statement: ISR-reachable code must not contain blocking calls, sleep, or busy-wait loops.
scope: core/foc/implementations/, core/state_machine/, ISR-reachable paths in targets/
mitigation: Reviewer traces call graph from ISR entry to confirm no blocking primitives (HAL_Delay, sleep, while(flag), etc.) appear.
violationConsequence: Blocking in an ISR halts the processor in interrupt context, preventing all lower-priority interrupts from firing and causing a system hang or watchdog reset.

statement: Every .cpp or .hpp file containing hot-path code must include the file-level pragma: #if defined(__GNUC__) || defined(__clang__) #pragma GCC optimize("O3", "fast-math") #endif
enforcement: Manual review by executor and reviewer agents.
mitigation: Reviewer checks the top of every hot-path file for the pragma block. Executor must add it to any new hot-path file.
violationConsequence: Without the pragma, the compiler uses the project-default optimization level (-Og in debug), producing significantly slower code that may miss the cycle budget.

statement: The OPTIMIZE_FOR_SPEED macro (from numerical/math/CompilerOptimizations.hpp) must be applied to Calculate(), Compute(), and all other hot-path methods.
mitigation: Reviewer checks that each Calculate() and Compute() definition is prefixed with OPTIMIZE_FOR_SPEED. Executor must add it when creating new hot-path methods.
violationConsequence: Same as i009 — method-level optimization attribute is required in addition to the file-level pragma for reliable hot-path optimization.

statement: All mock instances in tests must use testing::StrictMock<MockType>. NiceMock and NaggyMock are forbidden.
enforcement: Manual review. A grep for 'NiceMock|NaggyMock' in the test paths can be added to CI to automate enforcement.
mitigation: Reviewer greps for NiceMock and NaggyMock in any changed test file. Reject PR if found. Consider adding a CI grep gate.
violationConsequence: NiceMock silently swallows unexpected calls, hiding test failures. NaggyMock produces noisy warnings instead of failures. Both reduce test reliability and can mask regressions.

statement: Non-template class method implementations must not appear in header files. Only template class method bodies are permitted in .hpp files. All other implementations go in .cpp files.
enforcement: Enforced structurally by the Allman-style code review checklist. clang-tidy or a custom check could automate this.
mitigation: Reviewer flags any non-template method body found in a .hpp file as CRITICAL.
violationConsequence: Implementations in headers cause ODR violations when included across multiple translation units and increase compile times across the board.

statement: Classes and methods must use PascalCase. Member variables must use camelCase. Namespaces must use lowercase.
enforcement: Partially enforced by .ls-lint.yml for file names. Naming within code is enforced by reviewer agent checklist.
mitigation: Reviewer flags naming violations. The .ls-lint.yml configuration enforces file-level naming conventions in CI.
violationConsequence: Inconsistent naming reduces readability and makes automated tooling (grep, code navigation, agent context search) less reliable.

statement: All code must use Allman brace style with 4-space indentation.
enforcement: Enforced by .clang-format applied on save (see .vscode/settings.json) and in CI via the linters workflow.
mitigation: Run clang-format before committing. CI will reject improperly formatted code.
violationConsequence: Format inconsistency creates noisy diffs and makes code review harder.

statement: FOC code must use unit-typed aliases (Ampere, Radians, Volts, Rpm, PhasePwmDutyCycles, PhaseCurrents) instead of plain float.
enforcement: Manual review. No static analysis rule is currently enforcing the alias types.
mitigation: Reviewer checks function signatures and variable declarations in core/foc/ for bare float where a unit alias should be used.
violationConsequence: Plain float parameters are dimensionless; passing a value in the wrong unit (e.g., mechanical radians where electrical radians are expected) is a silent, hard-to-debug bug.

statement: All PID integrators must implement anti-windup via clamping or back-calculation.
enforcement: Verified by unit tests. TestFocTorqueImpl, TestFocSpeedImpl, TestFocPositionImpl must include anti-windup boundary tests.
mitigation: Reviewer checks that PID instantiation includes integrator limit configuration and that the corresponding test file includes an anti-windup/clamping test case.
violationConsequence: Integrator windup causes large transient overcurrent when the motor transitions from a saturated state, potentially damaging hardware or triggering fault states.

statement: Any change that alters a component's observable behavior must update the corresponding documentation/architecture/ or documentation/design/ document in the same change set. Code must follow documentation, not the opposite.
enforcement: Enforced by reviewer agent documentation checklist. No automated CI gate exists today.
mitigation: Reviewer checks that a documentation update accompanies every behavioral code change. Reject PRs where doc and code are out of sync.
violationConsequence: Documentation drift misleads future agents and developers, causing regressions when the stale doc is used as the source of truth for a new implementation.

statement: Visuals in architecture and design documentation files must be Mermaid code blocks or ASCII art. External image references (![alt](path)) are forbidden.
mitigation: Reviewer flags any img/png/svg reference in documentation files. Replace with a Mermaid block or ASCII art.
violationConsequence: External image references break in offline environments, CI doc checks, and AI agent reads. Mermaid is renderable anywhere.
